channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The Federal Reserve Could Embrace Prediction Markets
item.description: Fed economists praised Kalshi in a new working paper.

item.title: Who is OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger? The millennial developer caught the attention of Sam Altman and Mark Zuckerberg
item.description: Steinberger made $100M off his first company and quit coding. Now he’s back to show the world the “magic” of agentic AI.

item.title: Steve Jobs Saved Apple by Overcoming ‘Divestiture Aversion.’ It Will Work for You, Too
item.description: Divestiture aversion is both common and pervasive–and is probably holding you back.

item.title: The Hidden Threat Killing E‑Commerce Margins—and Costing Entrepreneurs Billions
item.description: As fraudulent returns and chargebacks surge, founders are developing tactics to protect their businesses.

item.title: Despite a $400 million net worth and Hollywood career, Reese Witherspoon tells young people to stop chasing their dreams—and do this instead
item.description: Reese Witherspoon is telling young people to stop romanticizing the “dream job” and start getting brutally honest about what they’re actually good at.

item.title: Ex‑SpaceX Engineer Unveils an $80 Plastic‑Free Coffee Maker as Microplastic Health Risks Rise
item.description: JC Foster’s startup is betting consumers will start caring about what their appliances are made of, not just what they do.

item.title: Bill Gates pulls out of India’s AI summit at the last minute in the latest blow to an event dogged by organizational chaos
item.description: The Microsoft founder's abrupt withdrawal, amid renewed scrutiny over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein, capped a chaotic few days for an event already dogg...

item.title: Brendan Fraser on resilience at work in your 50s: ‘The silences in a career can be deafening’
item.description: “Before The Whale , I had everything to prove. And now, to be honest, not so much,” Oscar winner Brendan Fraser, 57, told AARP The Magazine in an inte...

item.title: Elon Musk is pushing to build data centers in space. But they won’t solve AI’s power problems anytime soon
item.description: Proponents say orbital data centers could solve AI’s power and cooling crunch. Experts say that vision is far more distant than the hype suggests.

item.title: How Can You Spot a Bad Manager Fast? Look for This 1 Warning Sign
item.description: Don’t ignore it because when it happens, team morale will take a nosedive.

item.title: The case for being exclusive at work
item.description: Most leaders understand their message needs to define exactly who their work is for. Fewer realize that it should also define who it’s not for. Fewer ...

item.title: How hesitation is a fundamental brain feature, according to neuroscientists
item.description: At the Winter Olympics, skiers, bobsledders, speedskaters, and many other athletes all have to master one critical moment: when to start. That split s...

item.title: Google CEO Sundar Pichai says AI spending still makes sense despite bubble fears
item.description: The Google CEO touched down in New Delhi this week with a string of new India investments in tow.

item.title: I founded 1-800-Flowers.com 50 years ago. This is the secret to business success
item.description: Fifty years is a long time for any company to stay in business. About 20% fail in their first year. By year five, roughly half are gone. By the end of...

item.title: Change doesn’t fail by itself. It fails because people resist it
item.description: Change often fails and that rarely has anything to do with whether the concept is a good one or not. As Howard Aiken famously put it, “Don’t worry abo...

item.title: 8 ways you may be squandering money in retirement
You can invest in SpaceX before its IPO — but should you?
What oil hitting $70 a barrel would signal about U.S.-Iran tensions
‘Magnificent Seven’ stocks rise — but hardly enough to reverse a brutal February
U.S. stocks are trailing the world. It could be the beginning of an epic shift.
